response:
NO DOT USAGE HERE NOW
To find the equilibrium we set the rate of change to zero and solve for P using the given values of r and K and the average harvesting rate since the sine term oscillates around zero so the average h is 10
The equation becomes 0.1 times P times (1 minus P over 1000) minus 10 equals zero which simplifies to a quadratic equation yielding a positive equilibrium population of approximately 118 turtles when ignoring the oscillation or considering the mean state
When the carrying capacity decreases linearly as K(t) equals 1000 minus 3t the equilibrium condition changes to r times P times (1 minus P over K(t)) minus h equals zero
We must solve for P_e(t) at any time t by rearranging the logistic equation with the time dependent K and the constant average harvest of 10
The population becomes unsustainable when the maximum possible growth cannot offset the harvest which happens when the discriminant of the quadratic equation becomes negative or when the calculated positive root drops to zero
Setting the equilibrium population to zero in the equation r times P times (1 minus P over K(t)) minus 10 equals 0 implies that the term r times P times (1 minus P over K(t)) must equal 10
If we assume the harvest remains constant at 10 for the threshold calculation we find that the carrying capacity must drop to a critical value where the maximum sustainable yield equals the harvest rate
The maximum sustainable yield for a logistic model occurs at half the carrying capacity and equals r times K over 4
We set r times K(t) over 4 equal to 10 to find the critical K value which is 400 divided by 0.1 equaling 4000 but wait the harvest is 10 so rK/4 = 10 means 0.1K/4 = 10 so 0.025K = 10 so K = 400
Substituting K(t) = 1000 - 3t into K = 400 gives 1000 - 3t = 400 so 3t = 600 and t equals 200 years
At t equals 200 the carrying capacity is 400 and the population can no longer sustain the harvest of 10 turtles per year leading to extinction
The stability
